models.name: SliceWithWidth
models.doc: Result of [`slice_with_width`]; serialized to `{ text, width }`.

models.name: ExtractSegmentsResult
models.doc:
Result of [`extract_segments`]; serialized to
`{ before, beforeWidth, after, afterWidth }`.

models.name: StdinEventJs
models.doc:
One event emitted by [`StdinBufferCore::process`], mirroring pi's
`"data"` / `"paste"` EventEmitter channels. `kind` is `"data"` for a complete
input sequence (one keypress / escape sequence) or `"paste"` for the body of
a bracketed paste (markers already stripped); `value` is the payload string.

models.name: SessionCwdIssueJs
models.doc:
pi's `SessionCwdIssue`: a resumed session whose stored working directory is
gone. `sessionFile` is the persisted session path, `sessionCwd` the stored
(now-missing) directory, `fallbackCwd` the directory to continue in.

models.name: FuzzyMatchResult
models.doc: Result of [`fuzzy_match`]; serialized to pi's `{ matches, score }`.

models.name: InputEvent
models.doc:
Event surfaced by [`InputCore::handle_input`] so the JS shim can fire pi's
`onSubmit`/`onEscape` callbacks. `submit` is the submitted value (pi passes
the current value) or `null` when no submit fired; `escape` is `true` when a
cancel/escape fired.

ops.name: version
ops.doc:
Returns the crate version. Proves the native addon builds and loads.

Exported to JavaScript as `pidginNativeVersion`.

ops.name: expandPath
ops.doc:
`expandPath` (core/tools/path-utils.ts): fold unicode spaces, strip a leading
`@`, expand `~`, convert `file://`. Errors cross as thrown JS errors.

ops.name: resolveToCwd
ops.doc:
`resolveToCwd` (core/tools/path-utils.ts): resolve `file_path` against `cwd`.
Errors cross as thrown JS errors (pi's `resolvePath` throws on bad input).

ops.name: pathTryMacosScreenshotPath
ops.doc:
Private pi transform `tryMacOSScreenshotPath`, exposed so the shim's
`resolveReadPath` can rebuild pi's fallback ordering natively.

ops.name: pathTryNfdVariant
ops.doc: Private pi transform `tryNFDVariant`, exposed for the shim's `resolveReadPath`.

ops.name: pathTryCurlyQuoteVariant
ops.doc:
Private pi transform `tryCurlyQuoteVariant`, exposed for the shim's
`resolveReadPath`.

ops.name: parseKey
ops.doc: `parseKey` (keys.ts): decode a raw key sequence to its canonical id.

ops.name: matchesKey
ops.doc: `matchesKey` (keys.ts): does `data` decode to `key_id`?

ops.name: decodeKittyPrintable
ops.doc: `decodeKittyPrintable` (keys.ts): printable char from a kitty sequence.

ops.name: decodePrintableKey
ops.doc: `decodePrintableKey` (keys.ts): printable char from a key sequence.

ops.name: setKittyProtocolActive
ops.doc: `setKittyProtocolActive` (keys.ts): toggle kitty-protocol decoding.

ops.name: visibleWidth
ops.doc: `visibleWidth` (utils.ts): display width of a string, ANSI-aware.

ops.name: normalizeTerminalOutput
ops.doc: `normalizeTerminalOutput` (utils.ts): canonicalize ANSI/control sequences.

ops.name: truncateToWidth
ops.doc: `truncateToWidth` (utils.ts): clip to `max_width` columns, ANSI-preserving.

ops.name: wrapTextWithAnsi
ops.doc: `wrapTextWithAnsi` (utils.ts): hard-wrap to `width` columns, ANSI-preserving.

ops.name: sliceWithWidth
ops.doc: `sliceWithWidth` (utils.ts): slice `length` columns from `start_col`.

ops.name: extractSegments
ops.doc: `extractSegments` (utils.ts): single-pass before/after overlay split.

ops.name: findWordBackward
ops.doc:
`findWordBackward` (word-navigation.ts), default segmentation: cursor after
moving one word backward from `cursor` (UTF-16 index).

ops.name: findWordForward
ops.doc:
`findWordForward` (word-navigation.ts), default segmentation: cursor after
moving one word forward from `cursor` (UTF-16 index).

ops.name: parseGitUrl
ops.doc:
`parseGitUrl` (utils/git.ts): parse a git source string into pi's `GitSource`
JSON shape (`{ type, repo, host, path, ref?, pinned }`), or `null`. The shim
`JSON.parse`s the result.

ops.name: stripAnsi
ops.doc:
`stripAnsi` (utils/ansi.ts): remove ANSI escape sequences (OSC + CSI). The
shim keeps pi's non-string `TypeError` guard, so only strings reach here.

ops.name: getMissingSessionCwdIssue
ops.doc:
pi's `getMissingSessionCwdIssue`. Given the source's stored cwd and optional
session file (read JS-side from `SessionCwdSource`) plus the fallback cwd,
report a [`SessionCwdIssueJs`] when the source is a persisted session whose
stored cwd is non-empty and absent on disk; otherwise `None` (pi's
`undefined`).

ops.name: formatMissingSessionCwdError
ops.doc:
pi's `formatMissingSessionCwdError`: the human-readable error text for an
issue (backs the JS `MissingSessionCwdError` message).

ops.name: formatMissingSessionCwdPrompt
ops.doc:
pi's `formatMissingSessionCwdPrompt`: the interactive prompt text for an
issue.

ops.name: parseCommandArgs
ops.doc:
`parseCommandArgs` (harness/prompt-templates.ts): split an argument string
using simple shell-style single and double quotes.

ops.name: substituteArgs
ops.doc:
`substituteArgs` (harness/prompt-templates.ts): substitute prompt-template
placeholders (`$1`, `$@`, `$ARGUMENTS`, `${@:N}`, `${@:N:L}`) with args.

ops.name: getConfigValueEnvVarName
ops.doc:
`getConfigValueEnvVarName` (resolve-config-value.ts): the single env var a
value references, or `null` (pi's `undefined`).

ops.name: getConfigValueEnvVarNames
ops.doc:
`getConfigValueEnvVarNames` (resolve-config-value.ts): all distinct env var
names a value references, in first-seen order.

ops.name: isCommandConfigValue
ops.doc:
`isCommandConfigValue` (resolve-config-value.ts): whether a value is a
`!`-prefixed shell command.

ops.name: clearConfigValueCache
ops.doc:
`clearConfigValueCache` (resolve-config-value.ts): clear the process-lifetime
`!command` result cache.

ops.name: normalizeChangelogLinks
ops.doc:
`normalizeChangelogLinks` (utils/changelog.ts): rewrite inline markdown links
for a release. `version_json` is the JSON-serialized `string | ChangelogEntry`
the shim passes; a bare JSON string is a raw version, a JSON object is a
`ChangelogEntry`.

ops.name: comparePackageVersions
ops.doc:
`comparePackageVersions` (utils/version-check.ts): compare two semver
strings, mapping `Ordering` to `-1`/`0`/`1`. `None` (incomparable) crosses as
JS `null`; the shim converts it to `undefined` to match pi's `number |
undefined`.

ops.name: isNewerPackageVersion
ops.doc:
`isNewerPackageVersion` (utils/version-check.ts): is `candidate` strictly
newer than `current`?

ops.name: getProjectTrustParentPath
ops.doc:
`getProjectTrustParentPath` (trust-manager.ts): the nearest ancestor path, or
`null` at a filesystem root (pi's `undefined`).

ops.name: hasTrustRequiringProjectResources
ops.doc:
`hasTrustRequiringProjectResources` (trust-manager.ts): whether `cwd` carries
project-local resources that must be gated by trust. The shim passes pi's
`process.env.HOME || homedir()` as `home_dir`.

ops.name: fuzzyMatch
ops.doc:
`fuzzyMatch` (fuzzy.ts): fuzzy-match `query` against `text`, returning pi's
`{ matches, score }` (lower score = better).

ops.name: fuzzyFilter
ops.doc:
`fuzzyFilter` (fuzzy.ts): run pi's whole filter orchestration in Rust. Given
each candidate's already-materialized text and the query, return the
surviving candidates' original indices ranked best-match-first. The shim
maps these indices back to items, so pi's `getText` callback stays in JS.

ops.name: detectSupportedImageMimeType
ops.doc:
`detectSupportedImageMimeType` (utils/mime.ts): sniff a supported image MIME
type from magic bytes, or `null`.

interfaces.name: NodeExecutionEnvCore
interfaces.doc:
The Rust-backed host execution environment, exposed to JavaScript as
`NodeExecutionEnvCore`. The name avoids colliding with the shim's own
`NodeExecutionEnv` class, which composes this handle with a pi-original one.

ops.name: new
ops.doc:
Build a host env rooted at `cwd`, optionally with a custom `shellPath`
and base `shellEnv` (JSON object), mirroring pi's constructor options.

ops.name: cwd
ops.doc: pi's `cwd` field.

ops.name: absolutePath
ops.doc: pi's `absolutePath`.

ops.name: joinPath
ops.doc: pi's `joinPath`.

ops.name: readTextFile
ops.doc: pi's `readTextFile` (non-abort path).

ops.name: readTextLines
ops.doc: pi's `readTextLines` (non-abort path). `max_lines < 0` means "no limit".

ops.name: readBinaryFile
ops.doc:
pi's `readBinaryFile` (non-abort path). Raw bytes cross as a `Buffer`;
an error is thrown with a `{code,message,path?}` JSON reason.

ops.name: writeFile
ops.doc: pi's `writeFile` (non-abort path, string content).

ops.name: appendFile
ops.doc: pi's `appendFile`.

ops.name: fileInfo
ops.doc: pi's `fileInfo`.

ops.name: listDir
ops.doc: pi's `listDir` (non-abort path).

ops.name: canonicalPath
ops.doc: pi's `canonicalPath`.

ops.name: exists
ops.doc: pi's `exists`.

ops.name: createDir
ops.doc: pi's `createDir`.

ops.name: remove
ops.doc: pi's `remove`.

ops.name: createTempDir
ops.doc: pi's `createTempDir`.

ops.name: createTempFile
ops.doc: pi's `createTempFile`.

ops.name: exec
ops.doc:
pi's `exec` (non-streaming, non-abort path). `options_json` carries only
`cwd`/`env`/`timeout`; the result crosses as `{stdout,stderr,exitCode}`.

ops.name: loadSkills
ops.doc:
`loadSkills` (harness/skills.ts): traverse the given directories through
this host env and return `{skills,diagnostics}` as a JSON string. The
sourced/mapper variants are composed in the shim over this method, so no
opaque JS `source` value ever crosses the boundary. `disableModelInvocation`
is always present on each skill (see [`skill_to_value`]).

ops.name: loadPromptTemplates
ops.doc:
`loadPromptTemplates` (harness/prompt-templates.ts): load `.md` templates
from the given paths through this host env and return
`{promptTemplates,diagnostics}` as a JSON string. The sourced/mapper
variants are composed in the shim over this method.

interfaces.name: KeybindingsManagerCore
interfaces.doc:
The Rust-backed keybindings core, exposed to JavaScript as
`KeybindingsManagerCore`.

ops.name: new
ops.doc:
Build a core from pi's `definitions`/`userBindings`, each JSON-encoded as
an ordered array (`[{ id, defaultKeys, description? }]` and
`[{ id, keys }]`, `keys: null` for an explicit `undefined`).

ops.name: matches
ops.doc: pi's `matches(data, keybinding)`: does `data` match any bound key?

ops.name: getKeys
ops.doc:
pi's `getKeys(keybinding)`: the keys bound to `keybinding` (empty if
unknown).

ops.name: getConflictsJson
ops.doc:
pi's `getConflicts()` as JSON: an ordered array of
`{ key, keybindings }`.

ops.name: getResolvedBindingsJson
ops.doc:
pi's `getResolvedBindings()` as JSON: an ordered array of `[id, keys]`
pairs, in definition order. The shim rebuilds pi's `key | key[]` shape.

interfaces.name: CommandCore
interfaces.doc:
The Rust-backed package-manager command flow, exposed to JavaScript as
`CommandCore`. Holds one boxed [`CommandFlowMachine`]; the JS shim drives it
through [`CommandCore::start`] / [`CommandCore::advance`].

ops.name: new
ops.doc:
Build a command core for `op` from its JSON params (see the module docs
for the op catalog and param shapes).

ops.name: start
ops.doc:
Plan the first command (or finish immediately). Returns the JSON-encoded
`CommandStep` (`{ type: "run", request }` or `{ type: "done", result }`).

ops.name: advance
ops.doc:
Consume the JSON-encoded `CommandOutput` (`{ code, stdout, stderr }`) of
the last planned command and plan the next one (or finish). Returns the
JSON-encoded `CommandStep`.

interfaces.name: FauxCore
interfaces.doc:
The Rust-backed faux core, exposed to JavaScript as `FauxCore`.

The JS `registerFauxProvider` shim constructs one of these, keeps the response
queue in JS, and calls [`FauxCore::stream_resolved`] (or
[`FauxCore::empty_queue_result`]) per stream. Cross-call state (call count and
prompt cache) lives in the wrapped [`FauxProvider`].

ops.name: new
ops.doc: Build a faux core from pi's `RegisterFauxProviderOptions`, JSON-encoded.

ops.name: setNowMs
ops.doc:
Set the `now` (epoch milliseconds) the provider reads when stamping the
empty-queue/aborted message timestamps. The JS shim calls this with
`Date.now()` before each stream so those timestamps track JS time.

ops.name: api
ops.doc: The provider's api id (pi's `core.api`).

ops.name: modelsJson
ops.doc: The model catalog as a JSON array (pi's `core.models`).

ops.name: getModelJson
ops.doc:
pi's `getModel()`: the model with `id`, or the first model when `id` is
omitted. Returns `null` when no model matches.

ops.name: bumpCallCount
ops.doc:
Increment and return the call count (pi's `state.callCount++`). The JS
shim calls this before resolving a response factory, matching pi's order.

ops.name: callCount
ops.doc: The current call count without incrementing (pi's `state.callCount`).

ops.name: streamResolved
ops.doc:
Stream an already-resolved response message, returning the
`{ events, message }` result as JSON. The JS shim passes the message it
popped (or computed from a factory); Rust applies pi's clone + usage
estimate + delta streaming. `aborted` reproduces the pre-aborted signal
path (pi's `signal.aborted`).

ops.name: emptyQueueResult
ops.doc:
The result pi streams when the response queue is empty (an `error`-stop
message with usage estimated), as JSON.

interfaces.name: StdinBufferCore
interfaces.doc:
The Rust-backed stdin splitter, exposed to JavaScript as `StdinBufferCore`.

The JS `StdinBuffer` shim owns one of these, keeps pi's `EventEmitter` surface
and completion timer in TS, and per chunk: converts any `Buffer` to a string,
calls [`StdinBufferCore::process`], and replays the returned events onto the
emitter. All cross-chunk state (buffer, paste mode, pending Kitty codepoint)
lives entirely in the wrapped [`StdinBuffer`].

ops.name: new
ops.doc:
Build a buffer. `timeout_ms` mirrors pi's `StdinBufferOptions.timeout`
(default 10); it is retained for parity — the completion timer itself is
driven by the JS shim, so this value does not affect native splitting.

ops.name: process
ops.doc:
pi's `StdinBuffer.process`. Accumulate `data`, and return every event the
buffer produced for this chunk (complete `data` sequences and any
`paste`), in emission order. The incomplete remainder is retained and can
be read with [`StdinBufferCore::get_buffer`].

ops.name: flush
ops.doc:
pi's `StdinBuffer.flush`: emit the buffered incomplete remainder verbatim
as a list of `data` payload strings (empty when nothing is buffered), and
reset the remainder.

ops.name: getBuffer
ops.doc: pi's `StdinBuffer.getBuffer`: the currently buffered incomplete remainder.

ops.name: clear
ops.doc: pi's `StdinBuffer.clear` / `destroy`: drop all buffered state.

interfaces.name: InputCore
interfaces.doc:
The Rust-backed single-line input core, exposed to JavaScript as
`InputCore`.

ops.name: new
ops.doc:
Create an empty input core, wiring pi's `onSubmit`/`onEscape` seams to a
shared event cell that `handle_input` drains after each call.

ops.name: getValue
ops.doc: pi's `getValue()`: the current value.

ops.name: setValue
ops.doc: pi's `setValue(value)`: set the value, clamping the cursor.

ops.name: setFocused
ops.doc: pi's `focused` field setter — routed here because render reads it.

ops.name: handleInput
ops.doc:
pi's `handleInput(data)`: process a chunk of terminal input, returning any
`onSubmit`/`onEscape` that fired so the shim can replay it onto the JS
callbacks.

ops.name: render
ops.doc: pi's `render(width)`: render the input to a single line.

interfaces.name: SelectListCore
interfaces.doc:
The Rust-backed select-list core, exposed to JavaScript as `SelectListCore`.
Constructed with an identity theme and no `truncatePrimary` override; the
shim only builds one when pi's theme is identity and no override is set.

ops.name: new
ops.doc:
Build a core from pi's `items` (JSON array of `{ value, label,
description? }`), `maxVisible`, and the optional
`minPrimaryColumnWidth`/`maxPrimaryColumnWidth` layout bounds.

ops.name: setFilter
ops.doc: pi's `setFilter(filter)`: case-insensitive `value` prefix filter.

ops.name: setSelectedIndex
ops.doc: pi's `setSelectedIndex(index)`: clamp the selection into range.

ops.name: handleInput
ops.doc:
pi's `handleInput(keyData)`: move/confirm/cancel. Callbacks are handled by
the shim's original instance; the core only advances selection state.

ops.name: getSelectedItemJson
ops.doc:
pi's `getSelectedItem()` as JSON (`{ value, label, description? }`), or
`null` when the filtered list is empty.

ops.name: render
ops.doc: pi's `render(width)`: render the list to lines (identity theme baked in).

interfaces.name: TuiCore
interfaces.doc:
The Rust-backed differential renderer, exposed to JavaScript as `TuiCore`.

The JS `TUI` shim owns one of these, keeps pi's component tree / overlay /
focus / input logic in JS, and per frame: renders its components to lines,
pushes them via [`TuiCore::set_base_lines`], drives one render with
[`TuiCore::tick`], drains the write stream with [`TuiCore::take_writes`], and
forwards it to the JS terminal. The renderer's cross-frame diff state
(previous lines, cursor rows, full-redraw ladder) lives entirely in the
wrapped [`Tui`].

ops.name: new
ops.doc:
Build a renderer over an in-memory logging terminal of `cols` x `rows`.
`show_hardware_cursor` mirrors pi's `PI_HARDWARE_CURSOR` opt-in.

ops.name: setSize
ops.doc:
Update the terminal dimensions the next render reads (pi reads
`terminal.columns`/`rows` each `doRender`; the width/height-change full
redraw is decided inside `tick` by comparing against the previous frame).

ops.name: setClearOnShrink
ops.doc: pi's `setClearOnShrink`: clear empty rows when content shrinks.

ops.name: setTermux
ops.doc:
Model `isTermuxSession()`: on Termux, height changes do not force a full
redraw. The shim calls this per frame from `process.env.TERMUX_VERSION`.

ops.name: setImagesCapable
ops.doc:
Model terminal image capability (gates pi's `queryCellSize`). Not needed
for the base render diff, but kept for surface parity.

ops.name: setBaseLines
ops.doc:
Replace the entire base frame with pre-rendered `lines` (pi's TS
components rendered to `string[]`). Backed by a single line-buffer child.

ops.name: tick
ops.doc:
Drive exactly one render: `request_render(force)` then `flush()`. This is
the deterministic, timer-free equivalent of a single fire of pi's
coalesced scheduler (one `waitForRender()`). `force` resets the diff state
for a full redraw (pi's `requestRender(true)`).

ops.name: takeWrites
ops.doc:
Drain the accumulated write stream (pi's
`LoggingVirtualTerminal.getWrites()` + `clearWrites()`): the exact bytes
this frame emitted, then reset the sink.

ops.name: fullRedraws
ops.doc: Number of full redraws performed (pi's `fullRedraws` getter).

ops.name: cursorRow
ops.doc: Logical cursor row (end of rendered content).

ops.name: hardwareCursorRow
ops.doc: Actual hardware cursor row (pi's `hardwareCursorRow`).

ops.name: previousViewportTop
ops.doc: Previous viewport top (resize-aware cursor bookkeeping).

ops.name: maxLinesRendered
ops.doc: High-water working area (max lines ever rendered since last clear).
